title: P-N Junction Voltage Explorer
description: Interactive simulation of a silicon p-n junction under bias. A voltage slider sweeps from -5 V reverse bias to +0.75 V forward bias while the depletion region, fixed space charge, electric field, mobile carriers, and diode current respond in real time using the depletion approximation and the Shockley diode equation.
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: semiconductor physics
p-n junction
diode
depletion region
forward bias
reverse bias

educational.topic: P-N junction under forward and reverse bias
learningObjectives: Explain how forward and reverse bias change the depletion region width
Identify the space charge and electric field direction inside the depletion region
Apply the depletion-width equation W = W0 sqrt((Vbi - V)/Vbi) to predict readout values
Relate the exponential turn-on of carrier flow to the Shockley diode equation

pedagogical.interactionPattern: Parameter slider with real-time animated feedback
pedagogical.instructionalStrategy: Predict-test-observe: students predict depletion width and current before moving the voltage slider, then verify against live readouts
assessmentOpportunities: Predict depletion width at a given bias and compare with the readout
Record current vs. voltage in 0.1 V steps and plot on a log scale
Explain the field direction from the exposed ion pattern

controls.label: Voltage
controls.purpose: Applied bias voltage across the junction

controls.label: Start/Pause
controls.purpose: Start and pause the carrier animation; the simulation loads paused

controls.label: Reset
controls.purpose: Return to zero bias and re-initialize carriers

equations: W(V) = W0 sqrt((Vbi - V)/Vbi), W0 = 0.425 um
assumptions: Silicon step junction at 300 K with Na = Nd = 1e16 cm^-3
Ideal diode: no G-R current, series resistance, or breakdown
limitations: Carrier dots and crossing rates are qualitative, not to scale
Recombination is drawn a short fixed distance past the junction
